name: Ovens and the shores of the Tranco de Beas reservoir - circular through the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras N.P.
name_original: Hornos y las orillas del embalse de Tranco de Beas - circular por el P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
region_intro: Hiking around Hornos offers diverse trails through the mountainous terrain of the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. The landscape features include dense forests, significant elevation changes, and views over the Tranco de Beas reservoir. This region is characterized by its natural park environment, providing a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails in Hornos The most popular hiking route is Ovens and the shores of the Tranco de Beas reservoir - circular through the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras N.P., a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views of the reservoir and surrounding natural park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alto del Montero - circular through the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas, a difficult 11.3 miles (18.3 km) path. This trail leads through varied terrain within the natural park, featuring significant ascents and panoramic vistas. Local hikers also love the Hornos Castle and Village – Views of the Tranco reservoir loop from Hornos, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail leading through historical village settings and offering reservoir views, often completed in about 1 hour 48 minutes. Hiking around Hornos is defined by its mountainous forests, reservoir views, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy village loops to challenging natural park ascents. The routes in Hornos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 230 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Hornos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Hornos?
A: Hiking in Hornos, located within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, primarily involves mountainous terrain. You'll encounter dense forests, significant elevation changes, and trails offering views over the Tranco de Beas reservoir. The landscape is characteristic of a natural park environment, providing varied hiking experiences.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Hornos?
A: Yes, Hornos offers trails suitable for various ability levels. An excellent option for an easy hike is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21846883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos Castle and Village – Views of the Tranco reservoir loop from Hornos&lt;/a&gt;. This 3.9-mile (6.3 km) trail takes about 1 hour 48 minutes and leads through historical village settings while offering scenic reservoir views.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see along the trails in Hornos?
A: The Hornos area is rich in natural beauty and points of interest. You can explore the historic settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos&lt;/a&gt; itself, or discover natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2064086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Segura River&lt;/a&gt; and the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2792382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campos de Hernán Perea&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2305421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despiernacaballos Pass&lt;/a&gt; also offers impressive vistas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Hornos?
A: Yes, several circular routes are popular in Hornos. For a moderate option, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988207178/ovens-and-the-shores-of-the-tranco-de-beas-reservoir-circular-through-the-cazorla-segura-and-las-villas-sierras-n-p" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ovens and the shores of the Tranco de Beas reservoir - circular through the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras N.P.&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.4 miles (7.0 km) long. For a more challenging circular hike, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988193569/alto-del-montero-circular-through-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Montero - circular through the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas&lt;/a&gt; covers 11.3 miles (18.3 km) with significant ascents.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Hornos?
A: The routes in Hornos are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average score of 4.7 stars from over 230 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the scenic views of the Tranco de Beas reservoir, the diverse mountainous forests, and the historical landmarks found along the trails.

Q: Are permits required for hiking in the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park around Hornos?
A: Generally, permits are not required for hiking on established trails within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. However, for specific activities like camping, fishing, or accessing certain restricted areas, permits might be necessary. It's always advisable to check with the park's official information points for the most current regulations before your visit.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Hornos?
A: The best time to hike in Hornos typically depends on your preference for weather and trail conditions. Spring and autumn offer milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes, making them ideal for most hikers. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winters can bring colder temperatures and occasional snow in higher areas, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes in the Hornos area?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Hornos offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988193569/alto-del-montero-circular-through-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Montero - circular through the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas&lt;/a&gt; is an 11.3-mile (18.3 km) path with significant ascents and panoramic vistas. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38141972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos Viewpoint – Hornos de Segura loop from Camping Montillana&lt;/a&gt;, a 19.1 km route with considerable elevation gain.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Hornos?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for varied weather in the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, it's recommended to wear layers. Sturdy hiking boots are essential for stability on uneven paths. Depending on the season, bring waterproof outerwear, sun protection, and plenty of water. For winter hikes, warmer clothing and potentially traction devices may be necessary.

Q: Are there places to stay overnight or shelters along the hiking routes in Hornos?
A: While the guide focuses on hiking routes, the area around Hornos does offer options for overnight stays. You can find shelters such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3980308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo del Espino Shelter&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;C.F. El Campillo Shelter&lt;/a&gt;. There's also &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6373433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camping Llanos de Arance&lt;/a&gt; for those preferring a campsite.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Hornos?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes documented in Hornos, offering a diverse range of experiences from easy village loops to challenging natural park ascents. These trails have been explored by more than 1600 hikers using komoot.
tips: This town is located in Jaén, in the autonomous community of Andalusia. Its municipal term belongs entirely to the natural park of the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas.
